reason: The item cleanly tests boundary-aware admissibility judgments around cooling-selection claims, especially separating condition-specific results (immersion, nanofluids) from generalizable design constraints (uniformity/temperature limits). Decoys are largely single-fault and engineering-plausible, but some correct options (notably A and G) both push “exclude air cooling” with overlapping rationale, slightly reducing discriminative separation and increasing the chance of testwise patterning.
